claim: The zodiac section's anomalously low entropy (3.7149 bits, lowest of all sections) is explained by a label-oriented encoding in which the same small set of ~15â€“20 root words are repeatedly inflected and reused as astronomical labels, such that the top 20 most frequent words in the zodiac section account for over 55% of all word tokens in that section
evidence_for: Zodiac entropy (3.7149 bits) is the lowest of all sections, consistent with a restricted, repetitive vocabulary
Label sections in other medieval manuscripts use highly repetitive short word sets for month names, zodiac signs, and star names
12 zodiac folios with 1,297 words averages ~108 words per folio â€” consistent with short recurring captions rather than running prose
Existing high-confidence belief (0.763) already supports label-oriented encoding for zodiac/astronomical sections
evidence_against: Low entropy could also reflect a denser homophonic cipher masking fewer plaintext characters
Text-only section has highest entropy (3.9016) despite presumably being the most natural language â€” entropy alone may not distinguish label from prose
If zodiac labels are in an unknown language, the 55% coverage threshold is arbitrary
test: Extract all word tokens from the zodiac section (1,297 words). Rank words by frequency. Compute cumulative coverage: what percentage of tokens are covered by the top 10, 20, and 30 unique word types. Compare these coverage statistics to the herbal section (10,872 words) as a prose baseline and to the text-only section (2,349 words) as a high-entropy baseline.
test_metric: Cumulative token coverage of top-N word types for N=10,20,30; ratio of zodiac coverage to herbal coverage at each N
test_threshold: Confirmation: top-20 words cover >55% of zodiac tokens AND this ratio exceeds herbal top-20 coverage by >1.5x. Denial: top-20 zodiac coverage is within 10 percentage points of herbal top-20 coverage.

tests_run.test: extract all word tokens from the zodiac section (1,297 words). rank words by frequency. compute cumu
tests_run.details: Observed glyph entropy: 3.8627 bits. Expected for hypothesis: ~4.0 bits. Difference: 0.1373 bits.

reasoning: If a small lexical core dominates the zodiac section far more than in prose sections, it confirms a label-grammar hypothesis and would allow targeted plaintext guessing (month names, constellation names in Latin/Italian) against that core vocabulary, providing a potential crib attack entry point.
